print("\nBefore - key objects:")

    if n['color'] in ('W', 'O', 'p', 'G', 'M'):
        print(f"  {n['color']} id={n['id']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, pos={n['boundary'][0]}")
</parameter>
</function>

The positions are exactly the same - clicking the purple square didn't change anything! The only difference is the node IDs shifted (which is normal). So the mechanics are different from level 1.
Let me reconsider. In level 1, clicking objects moved them. But here, clicking the purple square did nothing. Maybe I need to click something else, like the gray diamonds or the rings themselves.
Looking at the layout again:
- White star at [4, 17]
- Orange square at [10, 23] with magenta center
- Purple square at [39, 48] with magenta center  
- Purple ring at [15-21, 54-60]
- Orange ring at [48-54, 37-43]
- Gray diamonds at [7, 49], [19, 8], [33, 45], [46, 54]
- Light gray trails connecting objects
Maybe the goal is to navigate the white star through the trails to reach a target? Or maybe I need to click the gray diamonds?
Let me try clicking one of the gray diamonds to see what happens.
